title: CSS Flexbox & Grid
description: Master modern CSS layout with Flexbox and Grid: axes, alignment, sizing, and when to reach for each.
author: DailyCoder

questions.prompt: In a flex container with `flex-direction: row`, which property aligns items along the main (horizontal) axis?
questions.explanation: `justify-content` aligns items along the main axis. `align-items` aligns along the cross axis. With row direction, the main axis is horizontal.

questions.prompt: What does the shorthand `flex: 1` expand to?
questions.explanation: `flex: 1` is shorthand for `flex: 1 1 0%`, meaning grow, shrink, and a zero basis so space is distributed purely by the grow factor.

questions.prompt: CSS Grid is a two-dimensional layout system, while Flexbox is primarily one-dimensional.
questions.explanation: Grid lays out content in rows AND columns simultaneously (2D). Flexbox handles a single axis at a time (1D), though it can wrap.

questions.prompt: Which Grid value creates as many equal columns as fit, each at least 200px, expanding to fill space?
questions.explanation: `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(200px, 1fr))` is the classic responsive grid: columns are at least 200px, and auto-fit collapses empty tracks so existing columns stretch to fill.

questions.prompt: Which properties can be used to align a single flex item differently from its siblings? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: `align-self` overrides cross-axis alignment per item, and `margin: auto` absorbs free space (e.g. pushing an item to one side). `justify-self` has no effect in Flexbox; `order` changes position, not alignment.

questions.prompt: In Grid, what does the `fr` unit represent?

options.text: A fraction of the remaining free space in the container
A percentage of the viewport width

questions.explanation: The `fr` (fraction) unit distributes the leftover space in the grid container after fixed-size tracks and gaps are accounted for.

questions.prompt: Which property defines the space between Grid rows and columns (the modern standard property)?
questions.explanation: `gap` (formerly `grid-gap`) sets the gutters between tracks. `gap` is now also supported in Flexbox.
